Hi Marilyn, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/JGNFRPL5 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

Hi Marilyn, Transform Credit is a cosigner-only lender, which means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. If you can't provide a suitable cosigner, we can cancel your application if you prefer. You can apply again anytime! You can reply CANCEL to this message, or use this link to continue when you are ready: https://tcrdt.com/s/QF8N14G2 Thanks, Transform Credit

[RE:A cosigner is your key to $5000] Hi Brian, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/6LX29ZFJ - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

[RE:A cosigner is your key to $7500] Hi Brian, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 700+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/6LX29ZFJ - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0619082633030807071631] Hi Kim,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $5000, on <DateCreatedCb>. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder’ email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Many thanks, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

[RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Rosalyn, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/W6VKYPPM - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

Hi Rosalyn, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/W6VKYPPM - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. Hi Stephen, To continue with your loan application, your cosigner will need to provide us with a longstanding checking account that is active, valid, in their name, is open at least 6 months, and capable of ACH payments. Accounts in a business name will not be acceptable. Please note that they cannot use a prepaid account or joint account with the borrower, and online banks such as Chime, Direct Express or GO2 Bank are not accepted since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies. If you need any further help, please let us know. Thanks, Michael@TogetherLoans

We just need you to find a suitable cosigner. If you are not able to find another cosigner, you can respond with CANCEL to close your application. Hi Steven, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/WQ5HZDC9 - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Together Loans

Hi Steven, a cosigner is someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a friend or family member. Suitable cosigners have a credit score of 750+ and a history of paying bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need a score as high as 750 and anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted. If you're unsure if your cosigner will be accepted, ask them to complete their application online, and they will be informed if they are accepted. If you need to provide a new cosigner, use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/WQ5HZDC9 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Together Loans

Hi Steven, thank you for your contact. Upon review, I could confirm we received your cosigner application, but Kristen did not complete it yet. We sent an email and SMS with a link so they can continue, you can also remind Kristen to check our messages. I hope this helps. Thanks, Together Loans

[RE:A message from Together Loans] Hi Danny,

Thanks for reaching out to us, we're more than happy to help you with any queries and questions that you have. 

We do have a phone number, +1 470 435 6300, this goes to our 24/7 interactive call handler. This service will give you live updates about your application, and how it is progressing through to payout! Calling this number allows us to give you instant information and saves you long waits to speak to a person.

We also have a really helpful section on our website with answers to our frequently asked questions: https://togetherloans.com/Help/Contact

If you question isn't answered by either of these services, you're welcome to reply to this email explaining your problem in detail and someone will get back to you as soon as possible. 

Thanks,
Jade@Together Loans

[RE:Nikie has taken the first step in helping you!] Hi Danny,

Unfortunately, your cosigner was declined because they failed to meet the criteria in our underwriting checks. However, this does not mean that you have been declined, and we can still get this loan paid out to you. We just need you to find a suitable cosigner, with strong credit and a history of making repayments on time. You can find helpful information regarding the cosigner requirements here: https://togetherloans.com/faq

Once you have found someone to be your cosigner, please send them this link to apply: https://tcrdt.com/s/HHO2QZOJ

If you are not able to find another cosigner, you can respond with CANCEL to close your application. You can re-apply at any time by visiting: https://togetherloans.com

Thanks,
Together Loans

[RE:Nikie has taken the first step in helping you!] Hi Danny,

We've got your $5000 reserved, you and your cosigner just need to finish your application by submitting your information.

Use this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/NLLGV64T

We will always do everything we can to make sure your loan is paid out as quickly as possible. We aim to pay out every loan within 24 hours of the cosigner being accepted (remember - your cosigner can be ANYONE you know who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time). 

If you and your cosigner complete the application online and all checks are completed without issue, we could get the money to you sooner than 24 hours. If we find that we need more information when we review your application, we'll be in contact - so make sure you're available to speak to us & follow any instructions that we send you as soon as possible.

In the meantime, you'll find helpful answers to lots of questions in our website FAQs: https://togetherloans.com/faq

Jade,
Together Loans

interactions.body:
[Request to cosign Tiffany Holiday’s Transform Credit Loan] Hi Milton, 

Tiffany Holiday is asking you to cosign their $5000 loan with Transform Credit. This means you trust Tiffany to make all of their loan repayments on time and in full but agree to make any repayments that Tiffany fails to make. We're able to lend to Tiffany based on this trust. 

Please follow the link below to provide your SSN, sign your agreement, and provide your card information, if you are happy to proceed. 

https://rgct.io/s/J16HDLOO

We will then arrange a video call to verify your identity, protecting you from identity fraud, and confirm that you are happy to cosign the loan. This will take 5 minutes to complete and can be done through: FaceTime, Skype, Zoom, Google Meets - whichever is easiest for you.

Thank you, 
Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0635040613342709091834] Hi Miranda,

Unfortunately, you did not meet our internal criteria for becoming a cosigner on this occasion.

Our underwriters complete several thorough checks to ensure a cosigner is a perfect fit for a borrower, your credit score is just one factor in this process.

However, please be assured that although your application was declined, Jacob can still continue their application with another cosigner and we will let them know of this. This will also not impact their application with us as we understand that sometimes it may take a few tries before finding the perfect fit.

Allen, TX 75013
Call Toll Free 888 397 3742
www.experian.com

I am sorry that you were not successful on this occasion.

Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0627072307052425030916] Hi Simone,
 
Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay your loan and that we should too! This trust allows us to lend to you even if you're been turned down elsewhere. 

A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners are more likely to be accepted). Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. 

Just send this link to your cosigner and they can register online in 2 minutes: https://rgct.io/s/4C5YXEF2 and if you have any more questions, please let us know.

Hi Marvin, 
Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. Unfortunately we cannot continue with Paulina as your co-signer.
At this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages, although this is something we are excited to develop in the future so that we can help more people that do not speak English. 
Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into. 
We appreciate your understanding.

You can use this link to invite someone new to co-sign https://tcrdt.com/s/QZEJ26FR
Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Anthony, We received your message about cancelling the loan application. I appreciate our loans may seem expensive, but in context, our interest rate is not high at all. Lending to customers who have bad credit or no credit at all is more risky and typically if you have a damaged credit score or no credit at all, there is no other option than pay-day lenders whose interest rate can be in the hundreds of percent. 
 
We offer a more flexible loan option and we don’t believe in hidden fees or charges. Additionally, a lot of our customers pay off the loans early when their financial situation improves, which saves them a lot of money in interest because our interest is calculated daily so you only pay for the time you borrow. You can settle early to save on interest, without the penalties that other companies charge, if you chose to do this.   
Thanks, Together Loans

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 060277CARD] Dear Kerry and Tibor,

In reference to our letter dated February 6th, please find attached a copy of Latrice’s contract as the original Creditor as confirmation that this is her loan.

Per your request, we have now ceased all direct communication with the client. 

Please note that this is a dual-liability loan with a co-signer. The monthly payment of $177.13 is due on the 1st of each month. At the moment, the loan is current, and the next payment is due on March 1st. If payment is not received in full and on time from Latrice, we will proceed with contacting and collecting from the co-signer under their separate agreement to make any to all payments Latrice fails. This is to prevent the full balance from becoming due and to avoid the risk of legal escalation against both parties. Additionally, to prevent a negative impact on their credit file, if the loan defaults, we will report this loan as delinquent 

Best,
Berginie
Head of Collections
Transform Credit

Hello Brandy. I'm sorry, but Averri wasn't a suitable cosigner at this time. We have a responsibility to make sure that cosigning your loan is something that they would be able to financially support and based on the information provided we don't feel that this is the case for them. But we've already accepted your application and want to help you get the money you need - you just need to find someone else to be your cosigner instead. A good cosigner has a credit score of 700+, and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners (not tenants) are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. To continue with your loan application, please use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/5VTLDQFR or let us know if you need more help. Thank you, Transform Credit

Hi Joseorlando. Thank you for reaching out. We understand this situation has been disappointing. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. However, I can confirm that at this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages. Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into.  We appreciate your understanding. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LVCWRUEQ Thanks, Transform Credit
